acceptanceCriteria: A task-tracker HTTP service exposes CRUD endpoints for tasks (create, list, get, update, delete).
Tasks persist to a local JSON-backed store with atomic writes.
Input is validated and invalid payloads return 4xx with a structured error body.
List supports filtering by status (open|done) and is covered by integration tests.
A status endpoint reports service health and the persisted task count.
End-to-end tests cover the create→list→update→delete lifecycle.
